subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Blue Giants: Fast and Furious
description: A fast breakdown of Blue Giants: Fast and Furious for builders.

hook.text: Blue giant stars are among the hottest and brightest in the galaxy. They burn through fuel at a reckless pace.

buildup.text: Surface temperatures exceed 20,000°C—more than three times hotter than our Sun.

discovery.text: Rigel in Orion is a blue supergiant 120,000 times brighter than the Sun, visible from 860 light-years away.

twist.text: Their brilliance is their downfall. Blue giants live only a few million years before exhausting their fuel.

climax.text: When they die, they often explode as supernovae—seeding space with heavy elements like iron and gold.

punchline.text: Burn bright, die young. Blue giants live fast and explode.

quiz.question: Why do blue giant stars have such short lifespans?
options: They burn through their hydrogen fuel extremely quickly
They are constantly losing mass to strong stellar winds
They are unstable from the moment they form
